posture: Watch — one fresh, material NSW-REZ development cleared the relevance bar in the 24 hours to Wednesday morning: EnergyCo has executed a Project Development Deed with Transgrid to upgrade the constrained South West REZ export path between Jerilderie and Wagga Wagga, with works now to get underway to unlock 3.56GW of connection capacity for the four projects holding South West REZ access rights (Origin's Yanco Delta, Spark's Dinawan Energy Hub, Someva's Pottinger Energy Park and BayWa.re's Bullawah Wind Farm). It is a concrete delivery and counterparty milestone that directly addresses the recurring South West REZ capacity/voltage constraint the radar has tracked — the deed scopes upgrading the 330kV Dinawan substation to operate at 500kV, converting the Dinawan-to-Wagga Wagga lines from 330kV to 500kV, and upgrading 11km of 330kV line from Gugaa to Wagga Wagga — and EnergyCo forecasts more than $17.8bn in private investment and ~1,690 direct construction jobs a year, following last month's $225m state acceleration package and a fast-tracked $60m community benefit tranche. Notably, this milestone lands two days after Squadron's 558MW Banandra project (logged 14 July) said it would bypass the at-capacity South West REZ and plug into the existing 330kV network — so the deed both firms delivery certainty for the four in-zone projects and sharpens the question of how out-of-zone generation reaches market. Everything heavier is a prior-digest carry-forward with no fresh movement in the window: the Draft New England REZ Access Scheme Declaration remains on public exhibition (released 7 July, feedback by 6 August, targeted end-September ministerial declaration) following EnergyCo's 14 July industry and community webinars; the New England corridor field-investigation program runs July-September across the refined 1km Muswellbrook-Walcha corridor to Bayswater (~150 affected landholders) amid live Walcha High Country Guardians opposition, the Coalition's late-June policy to scrap the 500kV corridor and cap the zone into the March 2027 election run-up, and a community-alliance disengagement threat; the Central-West Orana Port-to-REZ OSOM delivery campaign to Squadron's Uungula Wind Farm (underway from 13 July, ~700 loads over 12 months) and the Main Road No. 709 gazettal continue; and on Central-West Orana, ACEREZ's recommencement of access-track work under stronger heritage controls remains the latest word while the DPHI rock-shelter assessment stays open.
summary: A quiet scan surfaced one fresh, material NSW-REZ development: EnergyCo has signed a Project Development Deed with Transgrid to upgrade the South West REZ export link between Jerilderie and Wagga Wagga, unlocking 3.56GW of connection capacity for the four projects holding South West REZ access rights and directly targeting the zone's known capacity and 330kV/500kV voltage constraint. Works to lift the Dinawan substation to 500kV, convert the Dinawan-Wagga lines to 500kV and upgrade the Gugaa-Wagga line are now to get underway, with EnergyCo citing more than $17.8bn in expected private investment; it is logged as 'watch' as a de-risking delivery milestone whose execution EnergyCo will monitor. All other threads — the New England Access Scheme consultation window and its 14 July webinars, the July-September field-investigation program, the Walcha-area and Coalition opposition and the alliance disengagement threat, the Central-West Orana OSOM delivery campaign and Main Road No. 709 gazettal, the Banandra bypass proposal, and the ACEREZ rock-shelter matter — are prior-digest entries with no fresh movement in the window and are deduplicated out per the writing rules.

items.headline: EnergyCo signs a delivery deed with Transgrid to upgrade the constrained South West REZ export path, unlocking 3.56GW for four access-rights projects
items.summary: EnergyCo has executed a Project Development Deed with Transgrid to begin upgrades on the grid section between Jerilderie and Wagga Wagga that will lift the export capacity of the South West REZ and let 3.56GW of wind, solar and battery capacity reach major load centres. The scoped works include upgrading the 330kV Dinawan substation so it can operate at 500kV, converting the existing Dinawan-to-Wagga Wagga lines from 330kV to 500kV, and upgrading 11km of 330kV line from Gugaa to Wagga Wagga; the works enable the four projects that hold South West REZ access rights — Origin's Yanco Delta, Spark Renewables' Dinawan Energy Hub, Someva's Pottinger Energy Park and BayWa.re's Bullawah Wind Farm — to connect to the market. EnergyCo says the investment is expected to attract more than $17.8bn in private investment and support an average of about 1,690 direct construction jobs a year, and follows last month's $225m state acceleration package and a fast-tracked $60m community benefit tranche.
items.so_what: This is a concrete step from planning into delivery on the South West REZ's core structural weakness — the capacity and 330kV-versus-500kV voltage constraint that has repeatedly surfaced on the radar and that pushed Squadron's Banandra project to say it would bypass the zone entirely two days ago. Firming the export upgrade materially de-risks the connection pathway and revenue certainty for the four access-rights counterparties and strengthens the investment case for the zone, but it also shifts EnergyCo's exposure toward delivery-execution risk: the Dinawan 500kV conversion and line works are on the critical path for those projects' 2027-onward construction, so cost, schedule, outage-coordination and Transgrid-interface performance now become the things to monitor, alongside the unresolved question of how out-of-zone generation like Banandra reaches a market whose feeder lines toward Sydney remain limited.
